introduction.title: Introdução
introduction.mainGoal: Como líder de um império recém-fundado, seu objetivo é se tornar supremo sobre todos os outros. De [diplomacia](Clans) à [guerra](War%20Council), você precisa construir um império mais rico que os demais. Você disputará com centenas ou milhares de jogadores com a mesma meta.

aboutTurns.title: Sobre jogos por turnos
aboutTurns.description: Eixo do Mal é um jogo por turnos: há um limite de com que frequência seu império avança ou interage com outros.
aboutTurns.turnGeneration: Em Eixo do Mal, você recebe {{turnsCount}} turnos a cada {{turnsFreq}} minutos. Só é possível acumular {{turnsMax}} turnos de uma vez; o excedente vai para [turnos armazenados](Summary), até {{turnsStored}}; sempre que receber turnos extras, 1 turno armazenado é liberado para uso.
aboutTurns.turnUsage: Todas as ações em "Usar turnos" no menu lateral gastam uma quantidade variável de turnos. [Dinheiro](Cash), [fazenda](Farm) e [explorar](Explore) permitem definir quanto gastar; [construir](Build) e [demolir](Demolish) consomem turnos conforme a velocidade do seu império.
aboutTurns.combatTurns: Atacar no [conselho de guerra](War%20Council), enviar [ajuda](Foreign%20Aid) ou lançar um [feitiço](Magic%20Center) (em você ou em outro império) consome 2 turnos.
aboutTurns.freeTurns: Outras ações — [mercado negro](Black%20Market), [mercado público](Public%20Market), entrar ou gerir um [clã](Clans), [configurações do império](Empire%20Settings) ou mensagens no [correio](Mailbox) — não consomem turnos.
aboutTurns.maintenance: A cada turno usado, seu império cobra impostos e colhe comida. A receita mantém o império e o exército; a comida alimenta a população. Cuidado para não ficar sem dinheiro ou comida: o [banco](The%20Bank) ajuda no primeiro caso; o segundo pode ser desastroso.
aboutTurns.protection: Ao ser criado, seu império fica sob [proteção](Protection): outros não podem atacá-lo no início. Nesse período você também não ataca, não envia ajuda externa nem usa o mercado público. Depois de {{turnsProtection}} turnos, a proteção cai. Perto do fim da rodada, a proteção também é removida.

statusBar.title: Barra de status
statusBar.description: No topo e na base de cada página fica a barra de status, com as estatísticas principais do império. Detalhes estão no [resumo do império](Summary) e na [visão detalhada](Overview).

stats.turns: Quantos turnos você tem para usar. Histórico e próximo turno no [resumo do império](Summary).
stats.networth: Valor estimado do império. Define sua posição no [placar](Scores).
stats.land: Tamanho atual do império. Ganhe terras [explorando](Explore) ou [atacando](War%20Council).
stats.cash: Dinheiro em mãos, sem contar o que está no [banco](The%20Bank).
stats.food: Comida estocada. Sem comida, cidadãos e exército passam fome. Produza na [fazenda](Farm) ou compre no [mercado público](Public%20Market).
stats.runes: Quantidade de {{runeType}} dos seus {{wizardType}} para [feitiços](Magic%20Center). Gere mais [meditando](Meditate).
stats.health: Saúde e moral de cidadãos e exército. Melhore com [cura](Healing) ou ajustando a [alíquota](Cash).

otherIcons.title: Outros ícones
otherIcons.description: Abaixo da barra aparecem efeitos ativos no império, com tempo restante. Há links para o [correio](Mailbox) e [notícias do mundo](World%20News) com indicador quando há conteúdo novo.

newPlayer.title: Dicas personalizadas
newPlayer.protection: Você fundou um império em Eixo do Mal e está em proteção até usar {{turnsProtection}} turnos: ninguém pode atacá-lo. Use esse tempo para aprender e crescer.
newPlayer.newPlayerGuide: Se é novo, os tours e o guia ajudam muito: cada página tem um link para a seção certa. Tours existem em Construir e Conselho de guerra.
newPlayer.goal: Seu objetivo é fortalecer o império e dominar o mundo. Há vários caminhos: economia, exército, magia, comida — ou uma mistura.
newPlayer.magicTip: Mesmo sem estratégia mágica, construa alguns {{wizardBuilding}} para lançar feitiços no centro mágico: escudo, mudar era, portais do tempo, etc.

raceTraits.intro: Como {{raceName}}, seu povo é mais forte em {{strength}}
raceTraits.human: ser generalista. Você pode ir a qualquer lado; qualquer era serve.
raceTraits.elf: magia. Foque em torres de magos, use turnos em Meditar e feitiços no centro mágico. Melhor na era passada.
raceTraits.dwarf: indústria. Foque em forjas e turnos em Indústria. Melhor na era futura.
raceTraits.orc: indústria e militar. Forjas e Indústria. Melhor na era futura.
raceTraits.gnome: economia e mercados. Cabanas e mercados, turnos em Dinheiro, compras no mercado negro. Melhor na era futura.
raceTraits.troll: militar. Forjas, cabanas e mercados; use o bônus de ataque. Melhor na era futura.
raceTraits.drow: magia e militar. Torres de magos, Meditar, feitiços. Melhor na era passada.
raceTraits.gremlin: comida. Fazendas, turnos em Fazenda, venda no mercado público. Melhor na era presente.
raceTraits.goblin: indústria. Forjas e Indústria. Melhor na era futura.
raceTraits.hobbit: comida. Fazendas e turnos em Fazenda. Melhor na era presente.
raceTraits.ghoul: comida e indústria. Forjas e fazendas; Indústria ou Fazenda. Melhor nas eras presente e futura.
raceTraits.vampire: economia e indústria. Cabanas, mercados e forjas; Dinheiro ou Indústria. Melhor na era futura.
raceTraits.minotaur: militar e economia. Cabanas e mercados; use bônus de ataque e defesa. Melhor na era futura.
raceTraits.pixie: magia e economia. Torres, cabanas e mercados; Meditar ou Dinheiro; feitiços. Cuidado com ataque e defesa baixos. Melhor na era passada.

military.title: Unidades militares
military.description: O exército serve para defender o império e para atacar quem o ameaça. Tipos diferentes de unidade entram em [tipos de ataque](War%20Council) distintos; pode produzi-las na [indústria](Industry) ou comprá-las nos [mercados](Black%20Market).

armored.name: {{unitName}}
armored.description: Unidade base usada em ataques de guerrilha. Não é a mais forte, mas custa menos e pode ser mobilizada em massa para causar muitos danos ao inimigo.
armored.stats: Custo base ${{cost}}, poder ofensivo {{offense}}, poder defensivo {{defense}}.

land.name: {{unitName}}
land.description: Unidade forte de {{primaryStrength}} usada em cercos. Serve para conquistar terras aos inimigos.
land.stats: Custo base ${{cost}}, poder ofensivo {{offense}}, poder defensivo {{defense}}.

air.name: {{unitName}}
air.description: O ataque aéreo é por vezes a melhor opção; estas unidades também podem capturar terra em ataques especiais e têm vantagem em {{primaryStrength}}.
air.stats: Custo base ${{cost}}, poder ofensivo {{offense}}, poder defensivo {{defense}}.

sea.name: {{unitName}}
sea.description: Usada em assaltos costeiros e para enviar ajuda externa a outros impérios. Forte em ataque e defesa; é a unidade mais cara e a mais poderosa.
sea.stats: Custo base ${{cost}}, poder ofensivo {{offense}}, poder defensivo {{defense}}.

buildings.title: Estruturas
buildings.description: A terra do império pode ser dedicada a estes fins. Os edifícios podem ser [construídos](Build) ou [demolidos](Demolish) para mudar o foco:

housing.name: {{buildingName}}
housing.description: Os {{peasantName}} podem viver em terra vazia, mas estes edifícios são feitos para alojamento: cabem muito mais {{peasantName}}. Mais população significa mais imposto quando se usa o foco em [dinheiro](Cash).

economy.name: {{buildingName}}
economy.description: Fazem a economia crescer, aumentam a renda per capita e geram dinheiro diretamente. Essenciais na [estratégia de economista](Casher).

military.name: {{buildingName}}
military.description: Produzem as [unidades militares](Military); a percentagem de recursos por tipo de unidade define-se nas [definições de indústria](Industry).

barracks.name: {{buildingName}}
barracks.description: Reduzem despesas militares ao alojar tropas com mais eficiência, baixam o preço das unidades compradas no [mercado negro](Black%20Market) e aceleram o reabastecimento do mercado negro.

magic.name: {{buildingName}}
magic.description: Treinam e alojam {{wizardType}} e produzem {{runeType}} para lançar [feitiços](Magic%20Center). Essenciais na [estratégia de mago](Mage).

food.name: {{buildingName}}
food.description: Alimentam {{peasantName}} e o exército; sem comida a população e as tropas morrem de fome e desertam. Essenciais na [estratégia de fazendeiro](Farmer).

defense.name: {{buildingName}}
defense.description: Edifício puramente defensivo: por cada 1% de terra em {{buildingName}}, a defesa sobe 1% (até 50%) e a taxa de retornos decrescentes (DR) aumenta 0,01. Ajuda contra [ataques](War%20Council).

race.title: Raças
race.description: Há várias raças, cada uma com vantagens e desvantagens nas áreas abaixo:

offense.name: Ofensiva
offense.description: Poder ofensivo ao atacar outros impérios.

building.name: Construção
building.description: Velocidade para construir e demoler estruturas.

upkeep.name: Manutenção*
upkeep.description: Dinheiro pago por turno para manter as unidades militares.

magic.name: Magia
magic.description: Poder mágico ao lançar feitiços e quando outros lançam feitiços sobre si.

economy.name: Economia
economy.description: Renda per capita: quanto os cidadãos geram por turno.

exploration.name: Exploração
exploration.description: Quanta terra ganha por turno gasto a explorar.

market.name: Mercado*
market.description: Preços das unidades militares no mercado privado.

consumption.name: Consumo*
consumption.description: Quantidade de {{foodType}} que a população e o exército consomem por turno.

race.note: Percentagens positivas ajudam o império; negativas penalizam. Nos atributos com "*" o sinal pode parecer contraintuitivo: uma penalização de consumo de comida (negativa) aumenta a comida que as unidades precisam, enquanto um bónus de manutenção (positivo) reduz despesas.

era.title: Períodos de tempo
era.description: Os impérios existem em três eras. Interagir diretamente com outra era exige um [portal do tempo](Magic%20Center) aberto.
era.militaryNote: O poder ofensivo e defensivo das [unidades militares](Military) depende da era. Impérios em eras diferentes recebem estes bónus e penalizações:

economy.name: Economia
economy.description: Altera a renda per capita dos [cidadãos](Cash).

industry.name: Indústria
industry.description: Altera a capacidade de [produzir unidades militares](Industry).

exploration.name: Exploração
exploration.description: Quanta terra ganha por turno gasto a [explorar](Explore).

farmer.title: Estratégia de fazendeiro
farmer.description: Como fazendeiro, o foco é produzir comida e vendê-la a outros jogadores. A comida é muito procurada porque [tropas](Military) e [{{wizardType}}](Magic%20Center) consomem muito.

setup.title: Preparação
setup.raceRecommendation: As melhores [raças](Race) para agricultura são Gremlin e Goblin (menos consumo de comida, mais produção). Fazendeiros encaixam bem na {{presentEra}} pelo bónus de {{foodBonus}}% em comida. Use o [Centro mágico](Magic%20Center) para avançar de era o quanto antes.
setup.buildings: Concentre-se em dois tipos: [{{farmType}}](Build) e [{{wizardBuilding}}](Build). As {{farmType}} produzem comida; poucas {{wizardBuilding}} servem para mudar de era e lançar [{{spellShield}}](Magic%20Center) contra magia inimiga.

turnsUsage.title: Uso de turnos
turnsUsage.initialPhase: Comece com turnos cheios ({{maxTurns}}). Use turnos iniciais a [atacar](War%20Council); a saúde desce. Pare quando a saúde for inferior a 70% ou falhar um ataque e [construa](Build): cerca de 95% de {{farmType}}. Depois [Fazenda](Farm) até recuperar 100% de saúde. Repita até ficar sem ataques.
turnsUsage.foodProduction: Com muita terra e fazendas, use o resto do tempo a produzir comida. Lance [escudo mágico](Magic%20Center) antes de ficar sem turnos.
turnsUsage.selling: Venda o excesso no [Mercado público](Public%20Market) a preço razoável.
turnsUsage.profits: Quando voltar, alguém pode ter comprado a comida; o dinheiro estará no [banco](The%20Bank). Compre tropas e guarde caixa para despesas.

indy.title: Estratégia industrial
indy.description: Como industrialista, produza tropas e venda-as; outros jogadores precisam de exércitos.

setup.title: Preparação
setup.raceRecommendation: Melhores [raças](Race): Anão, Orc ou Goblin (bónus de indústria). A {{futureEra}} dá {{industryBonus}}% extra à indústria; [avançe para essa era](Magic%20Center) cedo.
setup.buildings: Foque [{{militaryBuilding}}](Build), [{{barracksBuilding}}](Build) e [{{wizardBuilding}}](Build). {{militaryBuilding}} cria tropas; {{barracksBuilding}} reduz manutenção e preços no [mercado negro](Black%20Market); {{wizardBuilding}} em pequena quantidade para eras e [{{spellShield}}](Magic%20Center).

turnsUsage.title: Uso de turnos
turnsUsage.initialPhase: Precisa de muita terra. Com turnos cheios ({{maxTurns}}), [ataque](War%20Council) até a saúde cair abaixo de 70% ou falhar; depois [construa](Build) ~85% de {{militaryBuilding}} e use [Indústria](Industry) até 100% de saúde. Repita até ficar sem ataques.
turnsUsage.troopProduction: Com terra e forjas, produza tropas. [Escudo mágico](Magic%20Center) antes de ficar sem turnos. Se faltar caixa ou comida, venda tropas no [mercado negro](Black%20Market).
turnsUsage.selling: Venda o excesso no [mercado público](Public%20Market). Preço demasiado alto afasta compradores; demasiado baixo reduz lucro; experimente lotes a preços diferentes.
turnsUsage.profits: Ao regressar, o dinheiro das vendas estará na [conta bancária](The%20Bank). Compre comida e reserve para despesas.

casher.title: Estratégia de economista
casher.description: Como economista, maximiza o dinheiro no império e compra comida e tropas nos [mercados](Public%20Market).

setup.title: Preparação
setup.raceRecommendation: Melhores [raças](Race): Gnomo, Vampiro e Minotauro (manutenção baixa, renda alta; bónus de mercado no Gnomo). A {{futureEra}} dá {{cashBonus}}% de rendimento; [avançe](Magic%20Center) cedo.
setup.buildings: Use [{{housingBuilding}}](Build), [{{economyBuilding}}](Build), [{{barracksBuilding}}](Build) e [{{wizardBuilding}}](Build). Cabanas trazem {{peasantName}}; mercados geram renda e renda per capita; quartéis reduzem custos e preços no [mercado negro](Black%20Market); torres para eras e [{{spellShield}}](Magic%20Center).

turnsUsage.title: Uso de turnos
turnsUsage.initialPhase: Precisa de terra. Com {{maxTurns}} turnos, [ataque](War%20Council), depois [construa](Build) ~45% de {{housingBuilding}} e {{economyBuilding}} cada; use [Dinheiro](Cash) até 100% de saúde. Baixar a [taxa de imposto](Cash) pode atrair {{peasantName}}.
turnsUsage.cashProduction: Com terra, edifícios e população, pode subir impostos para maximizar caixa (alguns camponeses saem). [Escudo mágico](Magic%20Center) antes de ficar sem turnos.
turnsUsage.spending: Gaste o lucro no [mercado público](Public%20Market) ou [mercado privado](Black%20Market); compare preços.

mage.title: Estratégia de mago
mage.description: Como mago, ganha ouro com feitiços.

setup.title: Preparação
setup.raceRecommendation: Melhores [raças](Race): Elfo, Drow e Pixie (magia e {{runeType}}). A {{pastEra}} dá {{runeBonus}}% extra em {{runeType}}; não é obrigatório mudar de era.
setup.buildings: Priorize [{{wizardBuilding}}](Build) para {{runeType}} e {{wizardType}}. Algumas [{{farmType}}](Build) ajudam porque os magos comem muito.

turnsUsage.title: Uso de turnos
turnsUsage.initialPhase: Precisa de terra. Com {{maxTurns}} turnos, [ataque](War%20Council), depois [construa](Build) ~95% de {{wizardBuilding}} e [Medite](Meditate) até 100% de saúde. Repita até ficar sem ataques.
turnsUsage.spellCasting: Com {{wizardType}} e mana, no [Centro mágico](Magic%20Center) lance [{{cashSpell}}](Magic%20Center) ao máximo. Compre comida nos [mercados](Public%20Market) ou use [{{foodSpell}}](Magic%20Center).
turnsUsage.spending: Gaste o ouro nos mercados; compare preços de comida e tropas.

protection.title: Primeiros turnos em proteção
protection.description: O império está protegido até gastar {{turnsProtection}} turnos. Não pode atacar nem ser atacado.

gettingStarted.exploring: Expanda terras a explorar; depois construa conforme raça e estratégia. Se faltar comida no mercado público, agricultura pode ser forte.
gettingStarted.magicTip: Construa sempre algumas {{wizardBuilding}} para mudar de era e lançar escudos contra magos.

leavingProtection.timing: Depois de {{protectionEndTurns}} turnos, espere voltar a ter {{maxTurns}} turnos antes de sair da proteção. Use sessões longas com turnos cheios.
leavingProtection.vulnerability: Fora da proteção pode ser atacado; também pode atacar — é a forma mais rápida de ganhar terra face à exploração.
leavingProtection.militaryTip: Focar um ou dois tipos de tropa ajuda; ataque e defesa mudam por era.

summary.title: Empire Summary
summary.description: This is the first page you will see each time you login. On it is displayed brief statistics for your empire and when you should expect to receive your next set of turns.

statistics.intro: In the center of the page, a table will display the following vital statistics of your empire:

empireInfo.name: Empire Name and Icon
empireInfo.description: Your display name, icon, and public profile that show on the Scores page. You can change your icon and profile text in the Empire Settings.

turns.name: Turns
turns.description: The number of turns you currently have available to use (and the maximum you are allowed to accumulate).

storedTurns.name: Stored Turns
storedTurns.description: The number of turns you have stored in reserve (and the maximum allowed). See the Introduction for more information.

rank.name: Rank
rank.description: Your empire's current rank among all other empires, based on its net worth.

population.name: {{peasantName}}
population.description: The current population of your empire.

land.name: Land Acres
land.description: The current size of your empire.

money.name: Money
money.description: The amount of money your empire has on hand, not counting any funds you may have stored in the Bank.

food.name: {{foodType}}
food.description: The amount of food your empire has stockpiled. Without food, your citizens and army will starve!

runes.name: {{runeType}}
runes.description: The amount of energy your empire's {{wizardType}} have available for casting spells.

netWorth.name: Net Worth
netWorth.description: The estimated value of your empire, taking all significant assets into account.

era.name: Era
era.description: The Time Period during which your empire exists.

race.name: Race
race.description: The Race of your empire's inhabitants.

health.name: Health
health.description: The health and happiness of your empire's citizens and army.

taxRate.name: Tax Rate
taxRate.description: Your empire's tax rate, which influences both the income of your empire's government and the happiness of its citizens.

military.name: {{armoredUnit}}, {{landUnit}}, {{airUnit}}, {{seaUnit}}
military.description: The number of units of each type your empire currently employs or maintains in its army.

wizards.name: {{wizardType}}
wizards.description: The number of spell casters your empire has at its disposal.

summary.roundInfo: Below this table is displayed the state of the current round (e.g. how long before it starts or ends), as well as the rate at which turns are given out and how long you should expect to wait before receiving additional turns.
summary.newsInfo: If any other empires have interacted with you recently, whether it be via the public market, or a rival empire attacking you, an indicator on the news clipping button will be displayed. Clicking the "Mark News as Read" link will dismiss the indicator.

overview.title: Detailed Overview
overview.description: This page provides detailed statistics about your empire and its economy, divided into 6 overall sections. The red or green numbers indicate how your stats have been effected by Race or Era modifiers.

turnsUsed.name: Turns Used
turnsUsed.description: This is the number of turns you have used since your empire was first created.

money.name: Money
money.description: Funds your empire has available on-hand.

rank.name: Rank
rank.description: Your empire's rank, determined by its networth, compared to other empires in the game.

netWorth.name: Net Worth
netWorth.description: Your empire's networth is calculated based on all of its available assets - acres of land, cash, {{foodType}}, your citizens, and your military - and provides a rough indication of how much your empire is worth.

population.name: Population
population.description: This is the number of {{peasantName}} that live in your empire. {{peasantName}} are necessary for making money to finance your empire.

race.name: Race
race.description: The Race of your empire's inhabitants.

era.name: Era
era.description: The Time Period during which your empire exists.

production.name: Production
production.description: {{farmType}} and unused land both help to produce {{foodType}} with which to feed your citizens and army. This number indicates approximately how much they will produce each turn.

consumption.name: Consumption
consumption.description: Your military, {{peasantName}}, and {{wizardType}} all require {{foodType}} to survive. This number shows your estimated consumption per turn.

net.name: Net
net.description: This number indicates whether you are gaining or losing {{foodType}} overall per turn. It is usually a good idea to keep an eye on this number, lest you run out and your people starve.

exploration.name: Exploration
exploration.description: How much land you will gain from one turn exploring

blackMarket.name: Black Market
blackMarket.description: Shows your racial bonus relating to price discounts or penalties on your Black Market

luck.name: Luck
luck.description: Luck is the percent chance to have a turn yield 1.5x production. This applies to new land when exploring, {{foodType}} when farming, income when cashing, troop production when doing industry, and {{runeType}} when meditating.

landDivision.title: Land Division
landDivision.description: Each row in this table indicates the number of each type of structure your empire has built on its land, as well as how many acres are currently unused.

perCapIncome.name: Per Cap Income
perCapIncome.description: This is your empire's per capita income, indicating how much money each of its {{peasantName}} make each turn. A percentage of this income is gained based on tax rate.

income.name: Income
income.description: Your empire's income is determined by the number of {{peasantName}} it has, its per capita income, its tax rate, and its overall health.

expenses.name: Expenses
expenses.description: Your empire's expenses consist mainly of military upkeep and land taxes. {{barracksType}} can help to lower these expenses.

loanPayment.name: Loan Payment
loanPayment.description: If you have borrowed any money from the World Bank, 0.5% of your loan is paid off each turn. Your loan payment for the next turn you take is indicated here.

net.name: Net
net.description: This indicates your empire's net income, whether it is gaining or losing money overall each turn. It is highly recommended to keep an eye on this value, lest you run out of money and are forced to take out a potentially expensive loan.

savingsBalance.name: Savings Balance
savingsBalance.description: This indicates how much money your empire currently has saved in the World Bank. Your account's interest rate is indicated in parentheses.

loanBalance.name: Loan Balance
loanBalance.description: Here is indicated the amount of money your empire currently owes to the World Bank. The loan's interest rate is shown in parentheses.

military.title: Military
military.description: The top rows indicate how many of each unit your empire currently has in its army.

offensivePower.name: Offensive Power
offensivePower.description: This number indicates your empire's total calculated offensive power (see Military Units for more information).

defensivePower.name: Defensive Power
defensivePower.description: Your empire's total calculated defensive power is shown here.

clan.name: Member of Clan
clan.description: If you are in a clan, its name is indicated here. If you are independent, this will simply say "None."

enemies.name: Enemies
enemies.description: If you are in a clan, clans you are at war with are listed here.

offensiveActions.name: Offensive Actions
offensiveActions.description: Indicates how many times you have attacked other empires, as well as the percentage of successful attacks (in parentheses).

defenses.name: Defenses
defenses.description: Indicates how many times your empire has been attacked by others, as well as the percentage of attacks that have been successfully resisted (in parentheses).

scores.title: Scores List
scores.description: This page lists the empires in the game. A pulsing blue dot indicates that a player is currently or was recently online. It is best to not interrupt players as they are using their turns so as not to spark a damaging conflict.

protected.name: Protected
protected.description: Empires in this color are protected from being attacked by other empires.

demo.name: Demo Accounts
demo.description: Empires in this color are demo accounts.

war.name: War
war.description: If your clan has declared war on another clan, its members will indicated by this icon.

locked.name: Locked
locked.description: These empires have been disabled by the Administration for violating the established Rules.

admin.name: Administrator
admin.description: These empires are responsible for keeping the game running smoothly. If you're experiencing technical difficulties playing the game, these are the people to talk to. To find bugs and test new features the admins may be active players in the game.

self.name: Yourself
self.description: Your own empire is always displayed in this color, making it easier to locate yourself in the score list.

statistics.intro: Each empire is listed in the table with the following statistics displayed:

rank.name: Rank
rank.description: The empire's rank (based on networth), compared to all other empires in the game.

empire.name: Empire
empire.description: The empire's icon, name, and identification number.

netWorth.name: Net Worth
netWorth.description: A calculated number indicating the empire's overall value.

land.name: Land
land.description: The total amount of land the empire occupies.

era.name: Era
era.description: The time period in which the empire exists.

race.name: Race
race.description: The race of the empire's inhabitants.

dr.name: DR
dr.description: The empire's current diminishing returns value. As an empire is attacked, their DR percentage increases which causes them to lose less land each successive attack. The rate of increase is affected by building guard towers.

scores.interaction: Click on an empire to view their public profile, time of last action, and to see options on how to interact with them directly from the scores page. You can see recent news from that empire, view or get new intel, send aid, an attack or cast a spell, all from the scores page.

clanStats.title: Clan Statistics
clanStats.description: This page lists all clans of sufficient size and provides various statistics - the clan's name, how many members it has, the average net worth of all members, and the summed net worth of all members.
clanStats.interaction: Clicking on a clan's name will list all of its members.

mailbox.title: Mailbox
mailbox.description: This section allows you to send private messages to other empires, whether to exchange information, forge alliances, or taunt your foes.
mailbox.moderation: There is a profanity filter on all messages but if you receive a message which you believe to be inappropriate or in violation of the game's rules, contact the Moderators and Administrators. Violators may have their empires Silenced or Disabled at the discretion of the Administration. Reports should only be used as a last resort - whenever possible, please attempt to resolve disputes personally so as to not place excess burden on the Administration.

worldNews.title: World News
worldNews.description: Here you can see news from all the empires in the game. News is categorized into Attacks, Spells, and Market events. You can search for events based on which empire is involved and the type of event.
worldNews.usage: Checking news is a valuable way to get insights into who you may want to attack or what is in demand on the markets.

farm.title: Agriculture Focus
farm.description: Here you may choose to spend time with extra focus placed on producing {{foodType}} to sustain your citizens and military.
farm.bonus: While spending turns here, your empire's gross production of {{foodType}} will increase by 25%.

cash.title: Economic Focus
cash.description: Here you may choose to spend time with extra focus placed on your empire's economy.
cash.bonus: While spending turns here, your empire's gross income will increase by 25%.
cash.taxRate: You can also adjust your empire's tax rate. A high tax rate will cause people to leave your empire and limit your maximum health but you will make more money per person. A lower tax rate will encourage people to move to your empire but you will make less money per person.

explore.title: Exploration
explore.description: For small empires, attacking others to gain land is infeasible - their time is better spent exploring.
explore.action: While spending turns here, your empire will expand its borders and gain additional land.
explore.warning: Be warned - as your empire grows larger, it will become more and more difficult to find usable land, at which point you will have to resort to attacking other empires to steal their land.

industry.title: Industrial Focus
industry.description: Here you may choose to spend time with extra focus placed on your empire's troop production.
industry.bonus: While spending turns here, your Blacksmiths troop production will increase by 25%.
industry.allocation: You can set the percentage of production to dedicate to each troop type. The total must add up to 100.

healing.title: Healing Focus
healing.description: Here you may choose to spend time with extra focus placed on healing your Military and {{wizardType}}.
healing.effect: While spending turns here, your empire's gross production of all resources will be 66% of their baseline value. In return you will gain 1 additional health point per turn up to 100%. Having a high health value is very important for successfully attacking and defending.

meditate.title: Meditate
meditate.description: Here you may choose to spend time with extra focus placed on your empire's {{runeType}} production.
meditate.bonus: While spending turns here, your {{wizardType}} {{runeType}} production will increase by 25%.

magicCenter.title: Casting Spells
magicCenter.description: From here, your {{wizardType}} can spend {{runeType}} and turns to cast spells on your own empire.
magicCenter.healthWarning: Spells cannot be cast if your empire's health is below 20%.
magicCenter.powerWarning: If your empire's magic power is not great enough, high level spells will result in a magical explosion and kill some of your {{wizardType}}.

shield.name: {{spellShield}}
shield.description: This will allow you to partially protect your empire from magical attacks from other empires. Casting once will protect your empire for 24 hours, and each additional cast will extend it by 3 hours. If your empire has less than 14 hours of protection remaining, it will automatically be renewed to 24 hours.

food.name: {{spellFood}}
food.description: This spell will create an amount of {{foodType}} to feed your citizens and army, the amount being proportional to the size of your empire and the number of {{wizardType}} at your disposal.

cash.name: {{spellCash}}
cash.description: This spell will create cash to fund your empire, the amount being proportional to the size of your empire and the number of {{wizardType}} at your disposal.

gate.name: {{spellGate}}
gate.description: This spell will open a Time Gate, allowing your empire to attack other empires in any time period. Casting once will open the gate for 12 hours, and each additional cast will extend it by 3 hours. If your empire's gate has less than 9 hours remaining, it will automatically be renewed to 12 hours.

ungate.name: {{spellUngate}}
ungate.description: If your empire currently has an open Time Gate, this spell will close it, protecting your empire from attacks by other empires in different time periods (unless they themselves have open time gates).

advance.name: {{spellAdvance}}
advance.description: This spell advances your empire from its current era to the next one (if such an era exists). At least 72 hours must be spent in your current era before you may cast this spell.

regress.name: {{spellRegress}}
regress.description: This spell regresses your empire from its current era to the previous one (if such an era exists). At least 72 hours must be spent in your current era before you may cast this spell.

build.title: Construction
build.description: From here, you may spend time constructing structures to increase your empire's land, economy, and military capabilities.
build.speed: Constructing structures is faster than demolishing (due to the ease of building), but some of the original costs cannot be recovered.
build.landReduction: Unused acres of land can also be dropped from your empire, though the need to relocate citizens and troops, not to mention the paperwork, makes this a slow process.

demolish.title: Demolition
demolish.description: From time to time, an empire may need to shift its focus to better cope with its situation - from here, you may spend time demolishing structures to make room for new structures to build.
demolish.speed: Demolishing structures is slower than building (due to the difficulty of disposing of the remains), but some of the original costs can be recovered.
demolish.landReduction: Unused acres of land can also be dropped from your empire, though the need to relocate citizens and troops, not to mention the paperwork, makes this a slow process.

favorites.title: Favorites
favorites.description: Add actions from the Use Turns or War Council pages to create your own custom page of most used actions. Streamline attacking, building, healing, etc by clicking the star on each page to add them to your favorites.

buying.title: The Black Market - Buying
buying.description: Within every empire, there exist those who train mercenaries, build instruments of war, and stockpile food. If your empire's {{militaryBuilding}} and {{farmBuilding}} do not produce enough to sustain its citizens and army, you may spend your money here to purchase these goods.
buying.replenishment: Only a finite number of goods can be purchased from the black market in a given time span - once depleted, you will need to wait for more to be produced. The rate at which units and food are replenished is based on both the overall size of your empire and how many {{barracksBuilding}} and {{farmBuilding}} you have, respectively.
buying.pricing: The cost of goods on the black market is affected by your economy and your ability to maintain such units - having a large percentage of your empire occupied by {{economyBuilding}} and {{barracksBuilding}} will reduce the purchase prices of military units by up to 40%; however, food prices are not affected.

selling.title: The Black Market - Selling
selling.description: Just as you can purchase from the black market, you can also quickly sell your excess goods to it, though for significantly lower returns.
selling.pricing: As is the case with buying, having a large percentage of your empire occupied by {{economyBuilding}} and {{barracksBuilding}} will increase the selling prices of military units to up to 50% of their usual purchase price.
selling.limit: A maximum of {{maxSellPercent}}% of each type of military unit can be sold on the black market in a given time span - in order to sell more, you must wait a while.

buying.title: The Public Market - Buying
buying.description: While the private market is convenient and reliable, its prices may be prohibitive if your empire does not focus strongly on its economy. Goods can be purchased from other empires via the public market, often at much better prices.
buying.bestPrices: Of all goods available on the public market, the least expensive will be shown to you. The identities of the empires you are purchasing from are not revealed to you, nor is your identity revealed to them.
buying.priceChanges: If less expensive goods become available while you are browsing, you will automatically purchase as many as possible at the reduced price before buying the rest at the price you originally were quoted. If somebody else buys goods while you are browsing, you will only buy what remains at the price you requested - if you require more units, you can purchase them separately at a higher price.
buying.ownGoods: When browsing, your own goods will never be listed for sale to you.

selling.title: The Public Market - Selling
selling.description: While the private market is a convenient place to rid yourself of excess food and equipment, you likely will not be well compensated. Using the public market, you can sell your goods to other empires willing to purchase them, often at much better prices.
selling.listingDelay: Once you place items on the public market, they will take {{startHours}} hour(s) to reach the marketplace and become available for sale.

removal.manual: Once items are available on the market, you will be allowed to remove them at your own discretion. Removing items from the market will result in only 75% of them being returned to you.
removal.automatic: Items are automatically removed from the market and returned to your empire after {{maxHours}} hours. Only 75% of them will be returned to you.

selling.priceEditing: You may edit the price of items currently on the market, however there is a 10% fee for doing so.
selling.warWarning: Be wary about selling during times of war, because the market coalition does not concern itself with inter-empire relations and will gladly sell your goods to your enemies.

lottery.title: Lottery
lottery.description: The lottery is a way to get a additional cash for your empire. You can buy three tickets per day. A random winner is chosen from the bought tickets but no winner is guaranteed. If there is no winner the money is added to the next lottery. The lottery is drawn once every 24 hours.

clans.title: Clans
clans.description: If you wish to ally together with a group of friends, you may form a Clan. Being in a clan offers several advantages; however, there are also downsides.
clans.defense: Clan members automatically deploy 10% of their troops for the sole purpose of defending other clan members - in doing so, clan members can significantly boost their defensive power against attacking empires. Beware - an enemy engaging in a Surprise Attack will be able to bypass these extra defenses, including the 10% you shared yourself!
clans.information: Clan members can see the combined news feeds, intel and stats of all empires in the clan as well as chat among themselves.
clans.war: However, being in a clan also opens the possibility of war, allowing members of enemy clans to attack you mercilessly (and also allowing your clan to do the same). War can be declared by another clan at any time. While at war you will be subject to a war tax. To end a war both clans must accept the peace offering.

joining.title: Creating and Joining
joining.creation: When you wish to create a clan, simply enter a short name and specify a password which other players must enter in order to join.
joining.joining: If you wish to join a clan which has already been created and you have been given its password, simply select the clan from the dropdown list and enter the password in the space provided.

warCouncil.title: Your Army
warCouncil.description: From here, you may deploy your empire's military to attack other empires and steal their land and resources. Once your empire reaches a certain size, this becomes the easiest method of expansion.
warCouncil.eraRestriction: In order to attack another empire, it must either be in the same [era](Era) as yours or a [Time Gate](Magic%20Center) must be open between your empires, either opened by you or by your target.

attackTypes.description: Six different attack methods are available for you to use, each having their own advantages and disadvantages. Guerrilla Strike, Lay Seige, Air Strike, and Coastal Assault will use one troop type for both the attacker and the defender. All Out Attack, Surprise Attack, and Pillage will use the entire army for both the attacker and defender.

guerrilla.name: Guerrilla Strike
guerrilla.description: By sending in only your {{armoredUnit}}, you can avoid your target's other forces. If successful, all structures on captured land are destroyed.

siege.name: Lay Siege
siege.description: By sending in only your {{landUnit}}, you can not only steal your target's land but also destroy additional structures on the land you do not capture. {{defenseBuilding}} and {{wizardBuilding}} are especially vulnerable.

air.name: Air Strike
air.description: By sending in only your {{airUnit}}, you can not only steal your target's land but also destroy additional structures on the land you do not capture. While attacking from above, significantly more structures can be destroyed.

coastal.name: Coastal Assault
coastal.description: By sending in only your {{seaUnit}}, you can not only steal your target's land but also destroy additional structures on the land you do not capture. {{defenseBuilding}} and {{wizardBuilding}} are especially vulnerable.

allOut.name: All Out Attack
allOut.description: The standard attack type, this allows sending all types of military units to attack your target. If successful, you will steal a percentage of your target's land, potentially with some of its original structures intact.

surprise.name: Surprise Attack
surprise.description: A surprise attack grants a 25% attack power bonus and allows the attacker to bypass any shared forces the defender's clan may have, though this comes at the cost of increased troop losses for the attacker as well as a significantly higher health loss. If successful, all structures on captured land are destroyed.

pillage.name: Pillage
pillage.description: Send your whole army on a raid for food and cash. If you are successful you will capture a small amount of land as well as steal food and cash from your enemy.

spells.title: Casting Spells
spells.description: From here, your {{wizardType}} can spend {{runeType}} and turns to cast spells on your enemies.
spells.healthRestriction: Spells cannot be cast if your empire's health is below 20%. Casting an offensive spell reduces health by 6%.
spells.powerWarning: If your empire's magic power is not great enough, high level spells will result in a magical explosion and kill some of your {{wizardType}}.

magicRatio.intro: To successfully cast an offensive spell, your magic ratio must be a certain value.
magicRatio.offense: Your magic offense power is: your {{wizardType}} / ((your land + enemy land ) / 2) * race magic bonus
magicRatio.defense: Enemy magic defense power is: enemy {{wizardType}} / enemy land * 1.05 * enemy race magic bonus
magicRatio.ratio: Magic ratio: your magic offense / enemy magic defense

blast.name: {{spellBlast}}
blast.description: If successful, this will eliminate 3% of your enemy's military and magical forces. If your target has an active {{spellShield}}, only 1% will be destroyed.

storm.name: {{spellStorm}}
storm.description: If successful, this will destroy a percentage of your target's cash and {{foodType}}. If shielded, it will only be 33% as effective.

runes.name: {{spellRunes}}
runes.description: If successful, this will destroy a percentage of your target's {{runeType}}, limiting the ability of their {{wizardType}} to cast spells. If shielded, it will only be 33% as effective.

struct.name: {{spellStruct}}
struct.description: If successful, this will destroy 3% of your enemy's structures. If your target has an active {{spellShield}}, only 1% will be destroyed.

fight.name: {{spellFight}}
fight.description: This spell will allow your {{wizardType}} to battle with your target's magic users. Successful attacks will steal approximately 33% as much land as a standard military attack.

steal.name: {{spellSteal}}
steal.description: If successful, this will allow you to steal cash from your target empire's treasury. If shielded, it will only be 33% as effective.

intelCenter.title: Intel Center
intelCenter.description: This section allows you to see and acquire information on other empires to find out their strengths and weaknesses. Intel can help you decide who to [attack](War%20Council) or [trade with](Public%20Market).

foreignAid.title: Sending Foreign Aid
foreignAid.description: If one of your friends or [clan mates](Clans) is in trouble and needs help, you can send some of your {{seaUnit}} with a shipment of troops and supplies. Up to {{maxCredits}} can be sent at any given time, and one additional shipment can be sent every {{delayHours}} hours.
foreignAid.limits: In a single aid shipment, you can send up to 15% of your empire's currently available [troops](Military), money, {{runeType}}, and {{foodType}} to another empire.

restrictions.netWorth: Aid can only be sent to those who actually need it - an empire whose [net worth](Overview) is significantly greater than yours likely has no need for your goods. However, if you are in a clan, you will be allowed to send goods to clan mates many times your size.
restrictions.war: It is not possible to send aid to those you are at war with. Check the [War Council](War%20Council) for current wars.

empireSettings.title: Empire Settings
empireSettings.profile: Here you can update your public profile and set an icon. Your profile is a way to describe your empire, play style, or just be silly. Your icon is another way to express your personality in the game. Both are visible on the scores page.
empireSettings.race: Change the race of your empire's inhabitants. This will come at a cost.

achievements.title: Achievements
achievements.description: Achievements are milestones your empire can earn throughout the round. Some achievements will be earned easily but others will require a dedicated effort. Achievements are saved to your user profile so you can earn all achievements over the course of multiple game rounds.

worldBank.title: The Bank
worldBank.description: The bank provides a place for empires both to store their excess funds and to take out loans during emergencies. View your current balances in your [Empire Summary](Summary).
worldBank.accountLimits: The maximum size of an empire's savings account and the most it can loan at once is determined mainly by the empire's [net worth](Overview).
worldBank.savingsRate: Savings account interest rates begin at {{bankSaveRate}}% and gradually decreases as your empire grows larger and begins to deposit more of its funds. Check your current rate in the [Overview](Overview).
worldBank.loanRate: Loan interest rates begin at {{bankLoanRate}}% and gradually increase as your empire grows larger and becomes more easily able to pay off loans. Check your current rate in the [Overview](Overview).
worldBank.emergencyLoan: If, while spending turns, your empire manages to run out of money, a loan will automatically be taken out for the amount you need, and the Bank will respect your state of emergency by allowing you to temporarily exceed your usual loan size limit by 100%. Consider using [cash focus](Cash) to earn more money.
worldBank.finalWeek: During the final week of a round, you will not be allowed to take out any additional loans. Make sure to check the [World News](World%20News) for round end announcements.
